skill.name: template-skill
skill.description: A minimal placeholder skill template for drafting custom AI skill instructions.
skill.summary: Start a new custom skill from a simple markdown template.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two duplicate high-severity weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md:3. Manual review found only placeholder description text and no cryptographic code, scripts, commands, network access, or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The duplicate static findings at SKILL.md:3 are false positives. The line contains placeholder description text, not a cryptographic algorithm or executable implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is a plain markdown frontmatter description. No hashing, encryption, imports, commands, or executable code are present in the scanned file.

content.value_statement: Teams need a simple starting point when creating custom AI skills. This template provides minimal frontmatter and a clear place to add instructions.
actual_capabilities: Provides a named SKILL.md file for a custom skill.
Includes frontmatter with the skill name and description field.
Provides a visible placeholder for adding operational instructions.
Can be adapted for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
Contains no scripts, dependencies, or executable automation.
limitations: Does not provide finished task instructions.
Does not include examples, tests, or supporting assets.
Does not automate skill validation or installation.
Requires authors to replace all placeholder text before use.

use_cases.title: Draft a New Internal Skill
use_cases.description: Use the template as the first file for documenting a repeatable assistant workflow.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Show new contributors the basic frontmatter and instruction layout used by a skill.

use_cases.title: Create a Marketplace Draft
use_cases.description: Start a community skill draft before adding detailed instructions and safety guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Begin a Skill Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template to draft a new skill for a repeatable task. Replace the description and add clear instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify Skill Purpose
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this template skill and propose a concise description, summary, and instruction outline for my use case.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Contributor Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this template into contributor guidance for creating consistent skills across our team.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Completed Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review a completed skill based on this template for clarity, scope, safety issues, and missing usage boundaries.

output_examples.input: Create a skill template for release note drafting.
output_examples.output: A concise skill outline with a release note description, instruction sections, expected inputs, and review boundaries.

output_examples.input: Improve this placeholder skill for team onboarding.
output: A short summary for marketplace listing.
Instruction headings for prerequisites, workflow, and output expectations.

best_practices: Replace placeholder text before sharing the skill.
Write instructions that define inputs, outputs, and boundaries.
Review the completed skill for prompt injection risks and unsafe commands.
anti_patterns: Publishing the template without replacing the placeholder description.
Adding broad instructions that let the skill handle unrelated tasks.
Embedding secrets, credentials, or private URLs in the skill instructions.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It provides a minimal markdown starting point for writing a custom AI skill.

faq.question: Is this a finished workflow?
faq.answer: No. It is a starter template that requires task-specific instructions.

faq.question: Does it run code or commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file contains only markdown frontmatter and placeholder text.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Skill authors, documentation maintainers, and teams creating reusable assistant workflows can use it.

faq.question: What should I change first?
faq.answer: Replace the description and add clear instructions for when and how the skill should be used.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
